description: arXiv:2607.13881v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Human-object interaction detection (HOID) has traditionally been formulated as a supervised detection problem over predefined interaction categories. While such paradigms achieve strong performance on closed-set benchmarks, they fundamentally entangle interaction understanding with dataset-specific supervision, limiting their ability to generali...
author: Ting Lei, Jialin Liu, Zhu Xu, Yuxin Peng, Yang Liu
